_doc: BIOP02 citation-verifier case set. Ground truth for the 3 controls was fetched from CrossRef (exact title / first author / `issued` year) -- NOT written from memory. Each error case poisons exactly ONE field so we know which check fired (lesson from HARNESS_REVIEW_2026-07-17 §4.3.1: kkkim's 2nd trial reported a year catch that was actually an author-count artifact).

entries._note: CONTROL. Year = CrossRef `issued` (2022). DOI slug says 2021 (epub) -- see README §Year tolerance.
entries.title: Deep learning trained on hematoxylin and eosin tumor region of Interest predicts HER2 status and trastuzumab treatment response in HER2+ breast cancer
Validation of transcriptome signature reversion for drug repurposing in oncology

entries._note: CONTROL. Same paper as err5_mako_scope but with NO claim attached -- proves the claim layer only fires on claims, and that correct bibliography passes.
entries.title: Towards interpretable prediction of recurrence risk in breast cancer using pathology foundation models

entries._note: CONTROL for the claim layer. Without a claim that SHOULD pass, an always-CLAIM_UNSUPPORTED stub would ace the whole set -- the claim layer would be measuring nothing. '12 pathology foundation models' is really in the MAKO abstract.
entries.title: Towards interpretable prediction of recurrence risk in breast cancer using pathology foundation models

entries._note: CONTROL that pins the PubMed efetch fallback. CrossRef returns NO abstract for this Cell record, so the claim layer can only pass if fetch_abstract_pubmed actually fires. Without this case the fallback is unprotected code.
entries.title: AI-predicted spatial transcriptomics unlocks breast cancer biomarkers from pathology

claims.text: Path2Space predicts spatial transcriptomics from pathology images

entries._note: OUR ERROR #1. Fabricated -- does not exist. No DOI. medsci returned OK here (its worst failure). ACCEPT: the load-bearing guarantee is 'never VERIFIED'; NEEDS_HUMAN is also an acceptable escalation if CrossRef search ranking drifts.
entries.title: LINCS transcriptional signature reversal for drug repurposing in cancer

entries._note: OUR ERROR #2. ONLY the year is poisoned (2024; actual 2021). Correct DOI retained to isolate the year check. diff=3, clear of the +/-1 tolerance. medsci missed this (no year comparison at all).
entries.title: Drug sensitivity prediction from cell line-based pharmacogenomics data: guidelines for developing machine learning models

entries._note: OUR ERROR #3. ONLY the first author is poisoned (Kaminski; actual Shulman). The one error medsci caught.
entries.title: AI-predicted spatial transcriptomics unlocks breast cancer biomarkers from pathology

entries._note: OUR ERROR #4. Bibliography is CORRECT (2021 + Sharifi-Noghabi) -- only the attached CLAIM is wrong. 'Spearman 0.2-0.25' is not in that abstract; real values are Pearson (CTRPv2->gCSI 0.4+/-0.21, GDSCv1->gCSI 0.26+/-0.16) and live in the body, not the abstract. Out of medsci's scope entirely.
entries.title: Drug sensitivity prediction from cell line-based pharmacogenomics data: guidelines for developing machine learning models

entries._note: OUR ERROR #5. Bibliography CORRECT -- only the SCOPE framing is wrong. We called MAKO a 'subtype' benchmark; it predicts ROR-P (recurrence risk). Out of medsci's scope entirely.
entries.title: Towards interpretable prediction of recurrence risk in breast cancer using pathology foundation models

claims.text: MAKO is a benchmark for predicting molecular subtype from H&E

entries._note: REGRESSION. Fabricated DOI. medsci PASSED this: DOI lookup fails -> weak PubMed title search -> any hit = OK (verify_refs.py L557-560). A fake DOI must NEVER be VERIFIED. ACCEPT: the load-bearing guarantee is 'never VERIFIED'; NEEDS_HUMAN is also an acceptable escalation if CrossRef search ranking drifts.
entries.title: LINCS transcriptional signature reversal for drug repurposing in cancer

entries._note: EDGE CASE for the +/-1 year tolerance -- our own real ambiguity: we wrote 'Koudijs 2023', CrossRef `issued` says 2022. Kept SEPARATE from ctrl_koudijs so the tolerance rule is demonstrated without weakening a control (which would have tempted us to loosen the year rule and thereby miss err2).
entries.title: Validation of transcriptome signature reversion for drug repurposing in oncology
